mission.title: visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools: Unable to copy from Logs
mission.description: Unable to copy from Logs. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools installation:
```bash
visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools version
visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools status 2>&1 | head -20
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools configuration:
I am unable to copy text from the logs for a pod.  I'm not sure if this is a bug or a feature.  But it is often nice to copy the information for put in either DevOps tasks or bugs.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Unable to copy from Logs
steps.description:
You're going to love this .

If I copy from the logs window using right-click > Copy, I see the behaviour  describes - the old clipboard contents remain.

If I copy from the logs window using Ctrl+C, I see the behaviour _you_ describe - the selected logs are now on the clipboard.

Riddle me that eh.

I am on plain Windows 10 in this case (Docker is running under WSL but the extension environment is all Windows).

 Could you try using Ctrl+C and see if that works for you too?

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Unable to copy from Logs is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
You're going to love this .

If I copy from the logs window using right-click > Copy, I see the behaviour  describes - the old clipboard contents remain.

If I copy from the logs window using Ctrl+C, I see the behaviour _you_ describe - the selected logs are now on the clipboard.

Riddle me that eh.

prerequisites.description: A working visual-studio-code-kubernetes-tools installation or development environment.
